commit.message:
[doc/testplan] Add BMC high-level test plan (#22688)

* [doc/testplan] Add BMC high-level test plan

Add the high-level test plan for SONiC BMC testing, covering:
- Overview of SONiC BMC and its role
- Test scope (in scope and out of scope features)
- Two new testbed topologies: bmc-dual-mgmt and bmc-shared-mgmt
- Testbed modeling with devices, console links, and topology definitions
- Embedded ethernet switch VLAN configuration for bmc-shared-mgmt
- Sniffer device for VLAN tagging verification
- SonicHost extensions: is_bmc() and get_bmc_host()
- Reused test suites and bmc-shared-mgmt specific test cases

Signed-off-by: Jing Kan <jika@microsoft.com>

* Address review comments: sniffer clarification and any marker handling

- Expand sniffer-01 description: clarify pass-through behavior, no impact
  on mgmt traffic or CPU testing, shared hardware with fanout support
- Add sniffer port modeling in testbed.yaml with dut_facing_port and
  mgmt_facing_port fields
- Add subsection on handling the any topology marker using the existing
  conditional mark plugin for BMC-incompatible test exclusions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Jing Kan <jika@microsoft.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Jing Kan <jika@microsoft.com>

commit.message:
fix(white-paper): close two remaining linguistic attack surfaces

1. Abstract/body: 'what Chomsky classifies as context-sensitive reading'
   -> 'what Chomsky's hierarchy classifies as context-sensitive'
   Chomsky classifies grammars and languages, not reading acts.

2. §4.2: Add usage note that syntactic/semantic are used in the
   programming-language sense throughout, not the formal linguistics
   sense. Closes the gap for any linguist who would object to the
   borrowed terminology being used with different referents.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(white-paper): add plain language summary after abstract

Adds a non-technical paragraph between the abstract and §1 for readers
outside software engineering. States the practical core: specify clearly,
add quality controls (decades of best practices plus case-specific gates),
automatic correction cycle closes the gap, system converges to a correct
program. You no longer need to write code; you need to specify.

commit.message:
fix(white-paper): linguistic precision fixes from philologist review

Six targeted corrections based on expert critique:

1. grammatical vs valid: Chomsky uses 'grammatical' not 'valid'. The
   paper now distinguishes Chomsky's structural notion (grammaticality)
   from GS's broader obligation (validity = grammatical + behaviorally
   conformant). The extension is explicit, not elided.

2. Load-bearing analogy: Added explicit mapping table — specification
   is the grammar, codebase is the language, implementation artifacts
   are sentences, architectural violations are ungrammatical strings,
   constraints are production rules, AI derivation is the generation
   process. States clearly what the analogy does NOT import (formal
   apparatus, derivation trees, pumping lemma).

3. generative capacity -> derivation precision: 'Generative capacity'
   in formal language theory is a property of the formalism type
   (Type 3 < Type 2 < Type 1), not of specific grammars. Replaced
   throughout with 'derivation precision', which names what the paper
   actually claims. Restriction glossary entry updated with explicit
   parenthetical clarifying the distinction.

4. Generative grammar definition: Previous definition described any
   formal grammar. Revised to capture what is specific to Chomsky's
   notion — recursive generativity, competence modeling, structural
   account of grammaticality. Explicitly states what formal apparatus
   is NOT being imported.

5. Context-free/context-sensitive glossary entries: Added explicit
   'used here as structural analogy' disclaimer so the analogical
   register is clear.

6. Footnote 2: Updated vocabulary list from 'valid' to 'grammatical'
   with a cross-reference to §4.1.a's explicit extension note.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Raise on failed individual asset generation to prevent incorrect cache updates (#1130)

* Initial plan

* Add error handling for failed image generation and tests to verify behavior

- In generate.py, each individual_* function now logs an error and raises
  an Exception when core does not produce the expected output file. This
  ensures the failure propagates up through generate_assets() so the asset
  type is not added to the successful_assets list and the cache is not
  incorrectly updated.
- Add tests/test_generate.py with 8 tests covering all four individual_*
  functions, verifying both the failure case (raises) and success case
  (no raise).

Co-authored-by: oscarlevin <6504596+oscarlevin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Initial plan

- Ran `poetry run black .` for formatting (reformatted test_generate.py)
- Added Fixed entry in CHANGELOG.md under [Unreleased]

commit.message:
fix: preserve session cwd across /new and session_expired

resetSessionUI() clears state.sessionCwd to null before
requestNewSession() reads it, so new sessions always fell back
to defaultCwd from config instead of inheriting the previous
session's working directory.

Capture cwd before calling resetSessionUI() in all affected
paths: /new command, session_expired event handler, and
connection resume fallbacks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Feat/saturn (#61)

* chore: update flatpakrepo URLs to castrojo fork

Point Url and Homepage to castrojo.github.io/testhub and
github.com/castrojo/testhub.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add io.github.kolunmi.Saturn

Saturn is a GNOME GTK4/libadwaita Flatpak launcher and application
manager by Eva M (kolunmi). Built with Meson (C) and a Common Lisp
backend via ECL (Embeddable Common Lisp).

Build notes:
- ECL compiled from source (tag 24.5.10) — first build is slow
- 15 CL library dependencies, all pinned to specific commits
- Runtime: org.gnome.Platform//50
- x-skip-install-test: true for bootstrap (remove after first index commit)

Broad filesystem permissions (--filesystem=home, /var/lib/flatpak,
--talk-name=org.freedesktop.Flatpak) are justified for a Flatpak
manager application.

Assisted-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 via GitHub Copilot
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(saturn): add finish-args-flatpak-system-folder-access lint exception

Saturn needs /var/lib/flatpak access (system-wide flatpak install dir)
since it's a Flatpak launcher/manager.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(saturn): rename app-id → id for flatpak-builder-lint exception resolution

flatpak-builder-lint's infer_appid() only reads the 'id' key, not
'app-id'. When appid cannot be inferred, exceptions are never applied
and all errors are reported unconditionally. Renaming to 'id' (the
canonical form accepted by both flatpak-builder and the linter) allows
our user-exceptions to take effect.

Root cause: flatpak_builder_lint/manifest.py infer_appid returns None
for manifests using the legacy 'app-id' key.

Assisted-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 via GitHub Copilot
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(saturn): add appstream-missing-screenshots builddir lint exception

manifest-lint uses 'metainfo-missing-screenshots', builddir-lint uses
the distinct key 'appstream-missing-screenshots'. Both needed for Saturn
since we have no upstream screenshots yet.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(saturn): add repo-stage screenshot lint exception

Three lint stages use three distinct exception keys for screenshots:
- manifest:  metainfo-missing-screenshots
- builddir:  appstream-missing-screenshots
- repo:      appstream-screenshots-not-mirrored-in-ostree

All three needed since Saturn has no screenshots yet.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(saturn): use app-id key instead of id

testhub's Justfile metadata recipe looks for 'app-id' key;
flatpak-builder accepts both 'id' and 'app-id' but testhub
convention (all other manifests) is 'app-id'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(justfile): support 'id:' as alias for 'app-id:' in manifest lookup

Newer Flatpak manifests use 'id:' as the canonical field (flatpak-builder
accepts both; flatpak-builder-lint's infer_appid() only reads 'id:').
Saturn uses 'id:' to allow user-exceptions to be resolved by app ID.

Updated all Justfile lookups to handle both keys:
- metadata recipe: falls back to '.id' when '.app-id' is empty
- _compile/_repack/run-test: yq '.".app-id" // .id' for direct reads
- grep-based directory lookups: '-lE (app-id|id): $APP'

All existing apps using 'app-id:' continue to work unchanged.

Assisted-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 via GitHub Copilot
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(saturn): remove x-skip-install-test bootstrap flag

Build 5 (run 23395028554) succeeded — Saturn is now indexed in the
testhub gh-pages repo. Real e2e install test can now run.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(skills): capture Saturn onboarding lessons

- Add saturn to app-gotchas.md (ECL time, three-stage lint exceptions,
  app-id key requirement, x-skip-launch-check, bootstrap pattern)
- Add two-build bootstrap pattern to pipeline.md
- Add three lint stages section to pipeline.md
- Add manifest key convention (app-id: vs id:) to pipeline.md

Lessons from Saturn CI run 23395028554 (feat/saturn branch).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(saturn): add x-skip-launch-check for headless CI

Saturn is a GTK4 GUI app — flatpak run exits 1 with 'Failed to open
display' in the headless gnome-50 container. x-skip-launch-check skips
the launch test while preserving the flatpak install verification.

Closes castrojo/testhub#6

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(pr61): address Copilot review comments

- grep -lE -> grep -lF (4 locations): app IDs contain dots which are
  regex wildcards in ERE mode; -F fixed-string avoids accidental matches
- testhub.flatpakrepo: revert fork-specific castrojo URLs to projectbluefin
- build.yml: remove non-existent e2e-install from container comment
- docs: update app-id vs id guidance in pipeline.md, app-gotchas.md,
  and flatpaks/saturn/GOTCHAS.md to reflect Justfile now supporting both keys

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: unify Jira + SNow bookmarklets into one 'Toolbox Relay'

The relay script already auto-detects Jira vs SNow via location.hostname —
there was never a technical reason for two separate bookmarklets.

- Renamed all bookmarklet links to '📎 Toolbox Relay'
- SNow Step 1 in CRG and PRB settings now points to the Jira bookmarklet
  instead of providing a duplicate drag target
- Removed cr-snow-bm and prb-snow-bm anchor elements from the DOM
- Removed dead references from crInitBookmarklets() and prbInitBookmarklets()
- tbxUpdateDragHints() simplified — no more separate snow hint IDs
- All Step 3 text updated to reflect unified bookmark name

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(cli): swallow terminal spawn errors gracefully — P8 smoke test passes

- openTerminal() now attaches error handler instead of crashing on ENOENT
- Warp/wt missing on non-Windows prints warning, does not exit non-zero
- P8 verified: Linear fetch → worktree → CLAUDE.md → agent spawn → log ✅

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Cerebro
repository.description: Cerebro is an Agent Orchestrator manages a fleet of specialized sub-agents, tools, and models to solve complex, multi-step problems. It specializes in decomposition, delegation, and synthesis
